intro: JavaScript provides an event-driven model optimized for real-time applications across client and server environments. Transitioning to PHP updates backends to an isolated, request-driven architecture designed specifically for rapid web rendering and CMS integration. Developers select this approach to build traditional web applications, use mature server-side ecosystems, or match standard hosting architectures.

diffs.from: Persistent long-running single process managing non-blocking loops.
diffs.to: Short-lived shared-nothing execution lifecycle spawning per request.
Dynamic with powerful progressive type-hinting support.
Variables require a leading dollar sign sigil prefix ($var).

diffs.from: Separate concepts for indexed arrays and structural objects.
diffs.to: Unified associative arrays used for collections, lists, and maps.

examples.title: Associative Object Lookup
examples.description: Creating a lookup map and accessing values safely.

examples.title: JSON Payload Formatting
examples.description: Converting structural values into serializable string formats.
examples.fromCode: const encoded = JSON.stringify({ ok: true, count: 1 });

tips.title: Enforce Explicit Type Hinting
tips.body: Add declare(strict_types=1); at the beginning of PHP files to catch incorrect parameter types early.

tips.title: Prefix Variables Correctly
tips.body: Remember to prefix all variable identifiers with a dollar sign ($) to write valid PHP syntax.

tips.title: Adapt to Synchronous Operations
tips.body: Write synchronous, sequential steps for standard file and database operations, as PHP does not use an event loop.

tips.title: Leverage Associative Arrays
tips.body: Use native associative arrays to handle both sequential lists and key-value dictionary structures.
